meta.scope_statement: Assess arxiv-mcp-server for researchers who already have a document store and email connectors.

executive_summary.posture: arxiv-mcp-server is a read-only, no-credential local server that fetches public arXiv papers into model context. Its own risk is low, but for the requesting population — who already hold a document store and email connectors — it adds the untrusted-content leg and completes the lethal trifecta. That composition risk, plus a name collision and an individual-maintainer supply chain, drive the rating; the conditions below reduce it.
conditions: Managed install only: package the pinned PyPI version internally and block ad-hoc npm/npx installs of this name.
Break the trifecta for the pilot: require confirmation on email-send in any session where read_paper has fired, or limit the pilot to users without the email connector.
Run a sandbox verification before rollout: confirm egress is arxiv.org only and record the tool-definition hash.

recommendations.action: Managed install only — publish the pinned PyPI version internally and block ad-hoc npm/npx installs of this name (name-collision defense).
Break the trifecta for the pilot population: gateway/client policy requiring confirmation on email-send once read_paper has fired, or restrict the pilot to users without the email connector.
Sandbox verification run before rollout: confirm egress is arxiv.org only; record the tool-definition hash for rug-pull monitoring.
Register in the inventory, wire definition-hash monitoring, and re-assess on version bump or in 12 months.

findings.title: Composition completes the lethal trifecta for the requesting population
findings.affected_component: read_paper (untrusted content) combined with the population's existing connectors
findings.description: For the requesting population (already connected: a document store and corporate email), this server adds continuous untrusted-content exposure — paper text enters model context — alongside existing private-data access and an email send channel.
findings.impact: A crafted paper could instruct the agent to read document-store content and email it out. Not a defect in this server; a property of the combination.
findings.likelihood: Moderate — requires a crafted paper to reach the agent, which is plausible for a research workflow.

evidence.detail: read_paper -> untrusted content leg; existing document store -> private-data leg; existing email -> external-comms leg. All three legs present for the population.

remediation.summary: Break a leg for the pilot: require confirmation on email-send after read_paper has fired, or scope the pilot to users without the email connector. Prefer a deterministic gateway control over relying on the model.

findings.title: Cross-registry name collision invites a supply-chain swap
findings.description: An unrelated third-party npm package exists under the same name. The project itself warns that installs must use `uv tool install` from PyPI, not npm/npx.
findings.impact: A user who 'npm installs' this name gets someone else's code — a straightforward supply-chain substitution.
findings.likelihood: Moderate — easy to hit by habit; the correct install path is non-obvious.

evidence.detail: Same name resolves to an unrelated npm package; upstream README warns: install via `uv tool install` from PyPI only.

remediation.summary: Managed install only: publish the pinned PyPI version internally and block ad-hoc npm/npx installs of this name.

profile.purpose: Bridges AI assistants to arXiv: search, download, and read paper content into model context, with local caching.
profile.license: Open source (verify current file at pin time)

capabilities.name: search_papers
capabilities.function: Query arXiv by keyword, author, or category

capabilities.name: download_paper
capabilities.function: Fetch a paper (HTML/PDF) to the local cache

capabilities.name: read_paper
capabilities.function: Load cached paper text into model context

permissions: Network: arxiv.org egress only by design (verify in sandbox)

hosting.install_time_behavior: Standard Python package; no fetch-and-execute observed in the install path.
hosting.data_residency: Local (public arXiv content plus local cache)

protocols.forward_compat_notes: No sessions; no Roots/Sampling dependence observed; low 2026-07-28 migration exposure.

authentication_and_credential_risk.notes: No credentials exist to steal; host compromise yields only cached public papers.

composition.analysis: For the requesting population (document store + email), read_paper adds continuous untrusted-content exposure alongside existing private-data access and an email send channel — the trifecta is complete. Standalone, the server is low risk.
composition.blast_radius: Whatever the pilot users' own document-store and email permissions reach; detection depends on email DLP and audit.

overrides_applied: Composition completes the trifecta for this population -> HIGH floor for that population absent conditions

limitations.disclaimer: Point-in-time static assessment (code review) of the package at pin time. Not a warranty. Findings require independent verification before remediation decisions rely on them.
limitations.verification_note: Findings are marked `reasoned` (static review); none were reproduced with a live proof-of-concept. The composition finding depends on the combined connected set. Run the validator skill to confirm.
